preview_text:
The model favors Morocco at 74%, with Haiti given a 5% chance of victory. The 69-percentage-point gap reflects a heavily asymmetric contest in which the underdog enters as a substantial longshot.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Morocco as commanding favorite, though the 21% draw probability suggests the match carries meaningful competitive uncertainty. A draw remains plausible; Haiti's path to victory is narrow but not impossible within the forecast range.

Model and sportsbook are tightly aligned on Morocco's win probability, differing by less than 1 percentage point. Both sources assign Haiti materially lower odds than the model does—roughly 4 percentage points lower on average across the three books—while crediting the draw at somewhat lower rates. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these minor divergences; both reflect extreme confidence in Morocco.
